summary: Mechanically split the large anonymous LB assembly file `asm/melee/lb/code_8001E2F8.s` into named LB module assembly files and updated `obj_files.mk` to build those modules separately. The diff shows no PR body or discussion, and the only review was an approval with no comment, so intent is inferred mainly from the title, file names, and diff structure.
what_changed: Renamed the old monolithic `asm/melee/lb/code_8001E2F8.s` file to `asm/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.s` in the diff, then removed most of its contents as those ranges were moved into separate LB assembly files.

Added `asm/melee/lb/lbgx.s` with the early `func_8001E2F8`/GX setup range shown in the diff excerpt, including calls such as `GXSetNumTexGens`, `GXSetNumChans`, `GXSetChanCtrl`, `GXClearVtxDesc`, `GXSetVtxAttrFmt`, `GXSetVtxDesc`, and `GXSetCurrentMtx`.

Added `asm/melee/lb/lbanim.s` containing animation-related assembly including `local_8001E560`, `local_8001E60C`, `func_8001E6D8`, and `func_8001E7E8`, with HSD AObj/JObj calls such as `HSD_AObjRemove`, `HSD_AObjAlloc`, `HSD_AObjSetFlags`, `HSD_AObjSetFObj`, `HSD_JObjSetFlags`, and `HSD_JObjClearFlags`.

Added `asm/melee/lb/lbmthp.s`, `asm/melee/lb/lbjpeg.s`, `asm/melee/lb/lbbgflash.s`, and `asm/melee/lb/lbrefract.s`; the diff includes embedded source-name strings such as `lbmthp.c` and `lbrefract.c`, supporting the chosen file names.

Updated `obj_files.mk` by replacing one object entry for `code_8001E2F8.s.o` with separate object entries for `lbgx.s.o`, `lbanim.s.o`, `lbmthp.s.o`, `lbjpeg.s.o`, `lbbgflash.s.o`, `lbrefract.s.o`, and `lbaudio_ax.s.o`.
The PR changed 8 files with roughly 6083 added lines and 6007 deleted lines, consistent with a relocation/split rather than a semantic rewrite.
smart_moves: Converted an address-based catchall filename into subsystem/module names, making later decompilation search and ownership easier.
Kept the split at assembly level rather than attempting C conversion at the same time, reducing behavioral risk and making the PR easy to approve.
Updated the build object list in the same PR, preserving build integration for the newly split assembly files.
Used evidence already present in the assembly, especially embedded source filename strings such as `lbmthp.c` and `lbrefract.c`, to identify module boundaries.
Preserved labels and addresses in the moved assembly, which helps maintain matching while improving file granularity.
decomp_lessons: Before decompiling a large unknown `code_<addr>.s` blob, split it into plausible source modules using address ranges, embedded filename strings, known symbol clusters, and subsystem call patterns.
For mechanical assembly splits, keep symbol names, local labels, constants, and section declarations stable unless there is clear evidence for renaming.
When splitting assembly files, update `obj_files.mk` in the same ordered location as the original object so link order and matching behavior are preserved.
Module names in `asm/melee/lb/` should align with known LB source names when available, such as `lbgx`, `lbanim`, `lbmthp`, `lbjpeg`, `lbbgflash`, `lbrefract`, and `lbaudio_ax`.
Small mechanical PRs that isolate file organization from decompilation logic are useful stepping stones for later C conversion.
naming_conventions: Replace anonymous address-based assembly filenames like `code_8001E2F8.s` with subsystem/module names when the module identity is known or strongly suggested.
Use lowercase LB module filenames under `asm/melee/lb/`, e.g. `lbgx.s`, `lbanim.s`, `lbmthp.s`, `lbjpeg.s`, `lbbgflash.s`, `lbrefract.s`, and `lbaudio_ax.s`.
Keep unresolved functions as `func_<address>` and local branch labels as `lbl_<address>` or `local_<address>` during a mechanical split; this PR did not try to invent descriptive C names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed a pure assembly split: large chunks were moved from the old monolithic file into new `.s` files while retaining original labels and instruction comments.
Preserved `.include "macros.inc"` and section structure in the newly added assembly files.
Preserved object link participation by replacing the old single object with multiple new objects in `obj_files.mk`.
Used existing embedded diagnostic/source strings such as `lbmthp.c` and `lbrefract.c` as boundary evidence for assigning code and data to module-specific assembly files.
No matching trick, C rewrite, or reviewer-requested codegen fix is visible in the provided PR context.
review_feedback: One review by `RevoSucks` approved the PR on 2022-08-05 with no written body.
No issue comments, review comments, or explicit reviewer guidance are present in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile `asm/melee/lb/lbgx.s` after this split.
Find later PRs touching `asm/melee/lb/lbanim.s` or functions `func_8001E6D8` and `func_8001E7E8`.
Search for PRs that convert `lbbgflash`, `lbrefract`, `lbmthp`, or `lbjpeg` from assembly to C.
Search for other PRs that split anonymous `code_<address>.s` LB assembly files and update `obj_files.mk`.
